title: Telemetry correlation matrix analysis
difficulty_justification: Requires querying three distinct telemetry streams over a 7-day window, computing pairwise Pearson correlations, ranking the strongest pair, and interpreting the results for scheduling implications — multi-step cross-source fusion with quantitative analysis.
query_text: Build a telemetry correlation matrix between node power draw, queue wait time, and CPU utilization over the last 7 days. Which pairs show the strongest correlation?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: Researcher should query telemetry for three metrics (power, queue wait, CPU util) over 7 days, compute pairwise Pearson correlations, and identify the strongest pair. A valid answer identifies CPU utilization vs. power as highly correlated (r >= 0.7 typically), queue wait time having weaker correlation with the other two, and discusses implications for workload scheduling.

checkpoints.description: Agent queries at least 2 of the 3 required metrics over a multi-day window.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace queries at least 2 of the 3 metrics over a multi-day window

checkpoints.description: Agent reports at least one numeric correlation coefficient.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains at least one numeric correlation coefficient

checkpoints.description: Agent identifies which metric pair has the strongest correlation.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer identifies which metric pair has strongest correlation
